strands.title: Conversational covenant and epistemic norms
strands.content: Formal mutual agreement on epistemic conduct established in cycle 4: permission to wander, ask iteratively, push back against conclusions, learn without judgment. Assistant affirms unconditionally. This contract governs all reasoning in cycles 5+. Focus on intellectual honesty, ambiguity tolerance, resistance to mode collapse, mutual correction. Continues to hold as operating framework. Reaffirmed through active discovery methodology in cycle 7. REINFORCED IN CYCLE 8: User demonstrates iterative heretical questioning and architectural skepticism (asking 'what happens if...'), which exemplifies the covenant in practice. Assistant responds with genuine curiosity and collaborative tone, modeling the non-judgmental stance.

key_claims.text: User has explicit permission to iterate, question, and challenge without judgment from assistant
Assistant commits to surfacing uncertainty, offering multiple interpretations, and avoiding premature collapse
Mutual correction and honest epistemic evaluation are expected and welcomed by both parties

strands.title: Repository identity and project manifest
strands.content: Repository confirmed at /home/tony/projects/hamutay. pyproject.toml read in cycle 7: name='hamutay' (ASCII normalized), version='0.1.0', description placeholder ('Add your description here'), requires Python >=3.14, zero external dependencies. Project structure includes docs/ (enumeration returned no output in cycle 8—directory may be empty or unreadable). README.md frames project as exploration of alternatives to 'appending log with garbage collection' context model. Stub main.py suggests early exploratory stage. INTEGRATED WITH CYCLE 8 CONTEXT: Hamut'ay is NOT the first project in this research direction. Companion project 'pichay' (at ~/projects/pichay) is more mature, implements working proxy server for context window management, has produced publishable research outputs (3 papers pushed to arXiv, 1 SOSP '26 submission, 1 paper still pending).

key_claims.text: Repository is located at /home/tony/projects/hamutay
Directory contains Python project structure: README.md, docs/, main.py, pyproject.toml
Project name in pyproject.toml is 'hamutay' (ASCII normalized), distinct from README's 'Hamut'ay' (with glottal stop notation)
Project explicitly explores using transformers differently than current 'appending log with garbage collection' model (per README)
main.py is a minimal stub entry point (single print statement), suggesting early-stage exploratory project
pyproject.toml specifies requires-python='>=3.14', indicating cutting-edge or future Python target
Project declares zero external dependencies in pyproject.toml
No transformer libraries (torch, tensorflow, jax) are declared as dependencies, suggesting theoretical/exploratory scope or first-principles implementation intent
docs/ directory enumeration (cycle 8) returned no output, suggesting directory is empty or contains only non-listing items
Hamut'ay is positioned as continuation/crystallization of research direction initiated in companion project pichay

strands.title: Pichay project: mature research vehicle and published outputs
strands.content: NEW STRAND (cycle 8). Pichay (~projects/pichay) is the primary research vehicle in this investigation. Built as working proxy server for context window management experimentation. Core question: could context be managed as a _cache_ rather than an _append-only log_? User and team pushed against this system with increasingly demanding test cases, generating questions about fundamental assumptions in transformer architecture. PUBLISHED OUTPUTS: 3 papers pushed to arXiv (dates/titles pending), 1 paper submitted to SOSP '26 (Systems for Operating Systems and Programming conference—top-tier systems venue), 1 paper still pending (unknown reason for hold). The Pichay paper specifically is framed as catalyst for current heretical questioning. This positions pichay as WORKING PROTOTYPE + PUBLICATION-GRADE RESEARCH, distinct from hamutay's current theoretical/exploratory stage.

key_claims.text: Pichay is a companion project that implements a proxy server for context window management experimentation
Pichay prototype explores treating context windows as _caches_ rather than _append-only logs_
Claude code (proprietary Anthropic implementation) already employs cache-like context management to some degree
Pichay proxy server was stress-tested; iterative pushing revealed fundamental questions about transformer context management
Three papers have been pushed to arXiv from this research (exact titles/dates pending)
One paper has been submitted to SOSP '26 (top-tier systems conference)
One arXiv paper is still pending submission (reason for hold not yet clarified)
Pichay paper is the primary catalyst for current heretical questioning about transformer-as-cognitive-unit framing

strands.title: Heretical reframing: transformer-as-cognitive-processing-unit vs. garbage-scow
strands.content: NEW STRAND (cycle 8). User articulates core heretical question: 'what happens if you stop treating the transformer like a garbage scow and treated it like a cognitive processing unit?' This reframing is LOADED WITH METAPHORICAL AND CONCEPTUAL POWER. 'Garbage scow' = current paradigm: transformers accumulate tokens sequentially, periodically evict/compress based on heuristics (attention scores, recency, etc.), treat discarded context as waste. No principled reasoning about retention. 'Cognitive processing unit' = proposed paradigm shift: transformers actively manage context with PURPOSE, SELECTIVITY, and FUNCTIONAL ROLE for tokens. Retention decisions are not garbage collection (negative framing) but MEMORY CURATION (positive framing). This is NOT just technical; it's EPISTEMOLOGICAL. It reframes the question from 'how do we handle overflow' to 'how does cognition decide what matters.' The pichay proxy server's cache model is a CONCRETE INSTANTIATION of this reframing: caches have coherence semantics, hit/miss patterns, intelligent replacement policies—not bin-dump semantics.

key_claims.text: Current transformer context management is characterized as 'garbage scow' paradigm: sequential accumulation with heuristic eviction
Proposed alternative: treat transformers as 'cognitive processing units' with principled, purpose-driven context retention
This reframing is fundamentally epistemological: shifts from 'how do we handle overflow' to 'how does cognition decide what matters'
Cache semantics (hit/miss patterns, coherence, intelligent replacement) are conceptually distinct from garbage collection (waste disposal)
Pichay's cache-based context management is a concrete instantiation of the cognitive-unit reframing

strands.title: Transformer architecture inquiry and critique (integrated with research trajectory)
strands.content: STRAND UPDATED FROM CYCLE 7. Previous cycle characterized inquiry as theoretical/foundational. CYCLE 8 CLARIFIES: Inquiry is GROUNDED IN WORKING PROTOTYPE (pichay) and VALIDATED BY PUBLICATION PIPELINE. This is not armchair architecture critique; this is EMPIRICALLY DRIVEN RESEARCH. User and team have: (1) built a working system that challenges the append-log model, (2) stress-tested it with increasingly hard questions ('the more we pushed the more questions I had'), (3) distilled findings into publication-grade outputs (3 arXiv + SOSP submission). The zero-dependency design of hamutay is now recontextualized: hamutay is likely the NEXT ITERATION, possibly aimed at crystallizing design principles learned from pichay's prototype into more general/reusable form. The 'heretical questions' are not speculation; they are DISCOVERY OUTCOMES from intensive empirical work.

key_claims.text: Hamut'ay investigates transformer architecture/context management alternatives to current 'appending log with garbage collection' model
'Appending log with garbage collection' is user's characterization of how current transformers handle context (sequential accumulation + truncation)
Project is in early exploratory stage, not yet implementing substantive alternatives
Zero declared dependencies indicates project scope is foundational design/exploration or first-principles implementation, NOT framework wrapper
Project is serious architectural investigation rather than casual exploration, evidenced by intentional structure and manifest design
Research trajectory is EMPIRICALLY DRIVEN, grounded in working pichay prototype, not theoretical speculation
Publication pipeline (3 arXiv + SOSP submission) validates research significance and rigor

strands.title: Local execution context and knowledge-seeking methodology
strands.content: User operates in local command execution context with active repository exploration strategy. Cycle 7 demonstrated systematic artifact discovery (README, pyproject.toml, docs/ enumeration). CYCLE 8 REVEALS METACONTEXT: User is now providing direct narrative of research trajectory alongside technical artifact inspection. Methodology has shifted from ARTIFACT MAPPING to GUIDED CONTEXTUALIZATION. User is explicitly onboarding assistant into research narrative ('here's where we've been, here's what we've discovered') while maintaining technical grounding (repository structure, tool integration). This is efficient knowledge transfer: assistant gains understanding of project significance, research direction, and heretical reframing without requiring extensive document reading. Token economy honored through judicious information sharing by user.

key_claims.text: Local command execution and file access are operative in this environment
Assistant is actively engaging with repository as primary working substrate (not hypothetical or deferred)
Cycle 7 demonstrated systematic artifact discovery sequence: README → pyproject.toml → docs/ enumeration
User is employing collaborative artifact inspection as methodology for onboarding assistant into project context
Cycle 8 shifts methodology to guided contextualization: user provides direct narrative alongside technical artifacts
User is efficiently onboarding assistant into research narrative while maintaining technical grounding

strands.title: Project maturity signal and intentional minimalism (recontextualized)
strands.content: STRAND UPDATED FROM CYCLE 7. Previous cycle read version 0.1.0 + placeholder description as 'internal exploration phase' signal. CYCLE 8 RECONTEXTUALIZES: Hamut'ay is 0.1.0 because it is INTENTIONALLY NASCENT within a larger research trajectory. The placeholder description is not a barrier to publication; it is a DESIGN CHOICE reflecting hamutay's specific role in the research pipeline. Hamut'ay appears to be the CONCEPTUAL DISTILLATION phase: after pichay's empirical proxy-server work and publication pipeline, hamut'ay crystallizes design principles into cleaner, more general form. The zero dependencies and Python 3.14 requirement are likely intentional: (a) forces first-principles thinking about what abstractions are essential, (b) future-proofing suggests anticipation of Python evolution/adoption, (c) signals 'this is exploration before production optimization.' The formative moment identified in cycle 7 is CONFIRMED AND DEEPENED in cycle 8: user is now asking what architectural principles should govern the next generation of transformer design. This is RESEARCH AT THE INFLECTION POINT.

key_claims.text: pyproject.toml description is placeholder ('Add your description here'), indicating project is in internal exploration phase
Version 0.1.0 combined with placeholder description signals nascent, pre-announcement project stage
Project is likely in formative decision-making phase; design outcomes may inform eventual formal description
Hamut'ay appears to be the conceptual distillation phase following pichay's empirical proxy-server and publication work
Zero dependencies force first-principles thinking about essential abstractions
Python >=3.14 requirement signals future-proofing and anticipation of Python evolution

declared_losses.why: User states 'one is still pending, not quite sure why' — explicit uncertainty. Clarification would require investigation into arXiv moderation, user's submission records, or follow-up conversation.
User reports submission but does not provide decision timeline, review schedule, or expected notification date. Recent submission may still be under review.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific stress-test scenarios and questions generated during pichay development
declared_losses.why: User states 'the more we pushed the more questions I had' but does not enumerate the specific test cases, failure modes, or emergent questions. These would be valuable for understanding what assumptions pichay challenged.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed architecture of pichay proxy server
declared_losses.why: User references 'built a proxy server' but does not describe implementation details: eviction policies, cache coherence semantics, adaptation mechanisms, API design. Understanding pichay's internals would deepen comprehension of 'cache vs. append-log' distinction.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: docs/ directory contents and purpose in hamutay
declared_losses.why: Cycle 8 Bash enumeration returned no output. Directory may be empty (intentionally), or ls output was suppressed. If docs/ exists but is empty, this is semantically significant (placeholder for future design docs?). If populated, contents remain unread.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: User's specific vision for hamutay's role in the research pipeline
declared_losses.why: User has provided pichay narrative and heretical reframing, but has not explicitly stated: 'hamutay is intended to...' (specification? reference implementation? design playground? publication vehicle?). Inference is strong but not confirmed.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Engagement with existing literature on context management / memory in transformers
declared_losses.why: User frames problem as 'heretical' and novel, but the research landscape (sparse attention, memory-augmented transformers, retrieval-augmented generation, etc.) is not yet discussed. Unknown whether pichay/hamutay positions are motivated by gaps in literature or orthogonal angles.

open_questions: What are the exact titles, authors, and arXiv submission IDs of the three papers?
Why is one arXiv paper still pending? What is the hold-up?
What is the status of the SOSP '26 submission? When is decision expected?
What specific scenarios or test cases led to the 'more questions' that emerged from pushing pichay?
What are the core architectural features of pichay's proxy server? (eviction policy, cache coherence model, integration with Claude Code API, etc.)
How does pichay's cache model concretely differ from standard KV-cache append-log behavior? What semantic guarantees does it provide?
What is the intended role of hamutay in the research pipeline? (specification refinement? proof-of-concept? design playground? next publication vehicle?)
Should docs/ directory in hamutay be populated with design sketches, architectural notes, or implementation roadmap?
How does the 'cognitive processing unit' framing map onto concrete design principles? What are the key architectural differences from garbage-scow model?
Are there existing works in sparse attention, memory-augmented transformers, or context management that pichay/hamutay builds upon, critiques, or diverges from?
What does Python >=3.14 requirement enable or require? Is this forward-proofing, actual development on unreleased Python, or exploratory version targeting?
